title: Employees With Missing Information
description: Find employee IDs who are in either Employees or Salaries table but not both.
constraints: employee_id is the primary key in both Employees and Salaries tables
An employee_id may exist in one table but not the other — no FK constraint enforced
Result must be ordered by employee_id ascending
key_concept: FULL OUTER JOIN via UNION of two LEFT JOINs in MySQL.

hints.L1: You need IDs that exist in one table but not the other. MySQL lacks FULL OUTER JOIN — use UNION.
hints.L2: NOT IN subquery for each direction.
hints.L3: SELECT employee_id FROM Employees WHERE employee_id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM Salaries) UNION SELECT employee_id FROM Salaries WHERE employee_id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM Employees) ORDER BY employee_id

optimal_solution:
SELECT employee_id FROM Employees WHERE employee_id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM Salaries)
UNION
SELECT employee_id FROM Salaries WHERE employee_id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM Employees)
ORDER BY employee_id;
